message:
perf(core): add memory pressure monitoring, inbox stats cache, and SmallVec optimizations

Multi-pronged performance infrastructure addressing the hotspot matrix
identified in the benchmark analysis:

mcp-agent-mail-core:
- config.rs: Add memory pressure thresholds (warning=2GB, critical=4GB,
  fatal=8GB) with MEMORY_WARNING_MB/CRITICAL_MB/FATAL_MB env overrides
- lib.rs: Export new memory module with MemoryPressure and MemorySample
- memory.rs: New module for runtime memory pressure detection and
  sampling, enabling adaptive behavior under resource contention
- lock_order.rs: Add DbReadCacheInboxStats lock level at rank 6 for
  the new inbox stats cache, shifting subsequent levels up by 1;
  COUNT bumped from 18 to 19

mcp-agent-mail-db:
- cache.rs: Add inbox_stats cache to ReadCache with get/put/invalidate
  operations; make cache capacity configurable (was hardcoded constant)
  with with_capacity() constructor; update all lru_evict_if_full
  functions to use parameterized capacity
- models.rs: Clarify InboxStatsRow documentation

mcp-agent-mail-tools:
- Cargo.toml: Add smallvec dependency for allocation-free recipient lists
- messaging.rs: Replace Vec with SmallVec<[T; N]> for recipient lists
  in send_message and reply_message hot paths — avoids heap allocation
  for typical 1-8 recipient messages while preserving Vec compatibility
  for larger fanouts via into_vec()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
